name: Lantern Docket
identity: Administrative-magical intake district where strange incidents are converted into casework.
approach: Visitors notice intake doors, waiting benches, and the unnatural calm of a place built to absorb panic.
inside: Inside, officials sort crisis into sequence, priority, and jurisdiction.
who_belongs: residents and specialists aligned with House Shirsh
who_is_out_of_place: outsiders who treat the locality as generic
seasonal_change: Traffic and tolerance shift with weather, trade, and ritual load.
law_context: Local expectations are enforced first socially, then institutionally.
sources: V33_1 parity completion synthesis from active polity summary and locality pattern matching.
canonical_identity: playability-support expansion preserving source-facing truth labels
scene_openers: Lantern Docket announces itself through the visible logic of filing work.
Within minutes, outsiders learn what House Shirsh considers normal here.
social_misstep_examples: cutting ritual, queue, or archive order
treating specialist labor as common labor
outsider_advantage: Fresh eyes can spot hidden strain before local actors admit it.
outsider_liability: A wrong assumption is remembered faster than a polite success.

arrival_variants_by_time.night: quieter, more suspicious, more dependent on personal trust

recent_local_change_signal: Locals insist nothing important has changed while adjusting to a new pressure pattern.
